title: Chapter 13: The Shadow's Messenger
content: The messenger arrived at dawn, riding a horse that was half-dead from exhaustion.
Sera was in the training yard with Kael when Darius brought the news — a rider at the eastern gate, wearing the colors of the Thornwood border patrol, with a message that couldn't wait.
"He's in the great hall," Darius said, his grey eyes grim. "You're going to want to hear this."

They found the messenger slumped against the high table, a young wolf named Bren who had been stationed at the eastern outpost for the past six months. His uniform was torn and bloodied. His eyes had the hollow look of someone who had seen things that couldn't be unseen.
"My Alpha." Bren's voice cracked. "The outpost. It's gone."
"Gone?"

"Everyone. Thirty wolves. They came at midnight — rogues, dozens of them, more than we'd ever seen. But they weren't like the rogues from the forest. These ones were... coordinated. Like someone was controlling them." Bren's hands were shaking. "They had a leader. A wolf in human form. He called himself Aldric's Voice."

*"Aldric. ALDRIC. He's making his move. Coordinated rogues. A 'Voice.' That means he's not just hiding in the shadows anymore — he's building an ARMY. Thirty wolves dead. THIRTY. They were under our PROTECTION."*
Kael's face was stone. "Did the leader leave a message?"

"He..." Bren swallowed. "He said to tell you that the exiled wolf sends his regards. And that he'll be taking back what's his. The pack. The castle. Everything. He said you have until the next dark moon to surrender, or he'll destroy Ironmoon the way he destroyed the outpost."
The next dark moon. The moonless night, when wolf powers were at their weakest. That was eight days away.
"He gave us a deadline," Sera said quietly. "He wants us to panic. To make mistakes."
"Or he needs eight days to prepare something." Kael's jaw was tight. "Something big enough to take the castle."
"Can he do it? With rogues?"

"The rogues aren't the real threat. They're foot soldiers. Cannon fodder." Kael turned to the window, staring out at the grey dawn. "The real threat is whatever Aldric has been doing for the past three years. He was exiled for dabbling in forbidden magic — old rituals that the pack outlawed generations ago. If he's spent three years perfecting them..."
"He could have something worse than rogues," Sera finished.
"Much worse."

*"The prophecy said 'the shadow.' It said 'the darkness that comes.' This is it. This is what the Goddess was warning us about. Aldric and his dark magic and his army of dead-eyed rogues. We're not ready. We need more time. Sera needs more time — her wolf is strong but she's only been training for a few WEEKS."*
"We have eight days," Sera said. "We can use that."
"For what?"
"To prepare. To train. To figure out what Aldric is planning and how to stop it." She met Kael's eyes. "And to finish what the prophecy started."
The next eight days were a blur of activity.

Darius organized the pack's defenses — reinforcing the castle walls, doubling the border patrols, stockpiling weapons and supplies. Magnus scoured the ancient texts for any mention of the dark magic Aldric might be using. The warriors trained from dawn until dusk, their forms shifting and flowing as they drilled combat patterns.
And Sera trained harder than she'd ever trained in her life.
"You're pushing yourself too hard," Kael said on the third day, watching her pick herself up from the training yard dirt for the dozenth time.
"I don't have a choice." Sera wiped blood from her lip. "Eight days isn't enough. It's never going to be enough. But I have to — "
"Rest."
"I can't rest while — "

Kael caught her wrist. His grip was gentle but firm. "You can't fight if you've broken yourself in training. Rest. Eat. Sleep. Then train more tomorrow." His golden eyes softened. "I'm not going to let you burn yourself out before the real fight begins."

*"She's exhausted. I can see it in her eyes. In the way she's holding herself — like every muscle is screaming. She's been training for HOURS. She needs to STOP. She needs to let US take care of her for once."*
"Fenris is worried about you," Sera said.
"Fenris isn't the only one."

She let him lead her back to the castle. His hand was warm around hers, steady and solid. The contact sent a familiar hum through her — the resonance of their linked wolves, growing stronger every day.

Magnus said the bond between them was accelerating. The more time they spent together, the more they trusted each other, the stronger both their wolves became. By the time Aldric attacked, they might actually be ready.
*Might.*
On the fifth night, Sera found Kael on the castle battlements.

He was standing at the edge of the wall, staring out at the darkness of the Blackwood Forest. The wind tugged at his loose hair. His military coat was absent — just a simple tunic, his shoulders bare to the cold night air.
"You should be sleeping," she said, coming to stand beside him.
"So should you."
"I couldn't."
"Neither could I."
They stood in silence for a moment, watching the stars. The moon was waning now — past full, sliding toward the dark moon that Aldric had named as his deadline.

"I keep thinking about the prophecy," Kael said. "'When the pack is broken and the shadow rises.' The pack isn't broken yet. Isolde's schemes failed. The trial unified us instead of dividing us. But the shadow is definitely rising."
"Maybe the prophecy wasn't predicting the pack would break. Maybe it was warning us what would happen if we didn't unite in time."
"The shadow drums," Sera quoted. "The howling of the shadow drums. What does that mean? What are shadow drums?"
"Magnus thinks it's a metaphor. The sound of an approaching army. The rhythm of dark magic being cast." Kael turned to face her. "But there's something else. Something I haven't told you."
Sera's heart clenched. "What?"

"When Aldric was exiled, he didn't just leave. He *cursed* me. Standing at the border, with the pack watching, he spoke words in the old tongue — words that burned themselves into the air like fire. 'May your heart be split in twain, nephew. May the wolf and the man war within you until one destroys the other.'"
"Your inner wolf," Sera breathed. "The split. It's not natural — it's a *curse*."

"I didn't realize it at first. I thought the inner voice was just... me. Stress. The weight of being Alpha. But it got louder over the years. More distinct. More separate. By the time you arrived, Fenris and I were barely speaking to each other."

*"We were at WAR. Not the kind with claws and teeth — the kind where two parts of the same soul hate each other so much they want to DESTROY each other. I wanted to be free. He wanted me to shut up. Neither of us knew how to fix it."*
"But you're getting better," Sera said. "You called him 'mine.' You joke about him. You're... integrating."

"Because of you." Kael's voice was rough. "The prophecy said you would bridge the divide. That you would heal the fracture. I thought it was metaphorical. But it's not — is it? The link, the mind-reading, the way you can hear both of us..."

"You needed someone who could see both sides," Sera said. "Someone who could love the cold Alpha and the ridiculous romantic wolf at the same time. Someone who could show you that both of you are worth loving."

Kael pulled her into his arms. The contact was fierce — almost desperate. "If we survive this — if we defeat Aldric — I'm going to break the curse. Not just for me. For good. I'm going to learn how to be whole."
"You already are." Sera pressed her face against his chest. "You just don't see it yet."
They stood together on the battlements, wrapped in each other and the cold night air, while the waning moon traced its slow path across the sky.
Three more days until the darkness came.
Three more days to become what the prophecy needed them to be.
